growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: silva.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Modified Frey's broth (mycoplasma broth, MB) + 10% swine serum

up.method: GC-MS of culture supernatant, late-log cells in fresh MB, 0-240 min (Fig 5A)
[U-13C]glucose (1 mM) labeling, GC-MS of intracellular metabolites (Fig 4A)

sec.units: fold-change relative to t=0 (GC-MS peak abundance)
sec.method: GC-MS of culture supernatant, late-log cells in fresh MB, 0-240 min (Fig 5C)

mfa.note: Rapid glucose uptake and glycolytic/PPP flux; M+3 and M+5 ions for adenosine/AMP indicate active purine salvage. No absolute mmol/gDW/h.
Rapid sugar fermentation with lactate excreted as waste product; label not incorporated into lactate/malate from glucose (LDH/MDH MGA_0746 inactive). Directional only.
Confirms absence of functional TCA cycle; fumarate/malate/citrate likely scavenged from medium.

growth_records.cite: Masukagami Y et al. Comparative Metabolomics of Mycoplasma bovis and Mycoplasma gallisepticum Reveals Fundamental Differences in Active Metabolic Pathways and Suggests Novel Gene Annotations. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00055-17. [PMC5634790]
growth_records.method: not_reported (qualitative vs prior growth curves; phenol-red MPN/CCU; no mu or td value)
growth_records.snippet: M. gallisepticum rapidly depleted the culture medium of both fructose and glucose, whereas lactate levels rose by more than 5-fold after 30 min, suggesting rapid sugar fermentation and secretion of lactate as a waste product... [13C]glucose
growth_records.notes: 13C-tracer/metabolomics paper; NO absolute rates (mmol/gDW/h), growth rate, doubling time, or yields reported; cell numbers standardized to 1x10^10 CCU. Fig 5 depletion/secretion values are GC-MS relative abundances vs t=0 (lactate >5-fold at 30 min is the only semi-quantitative secretion number); 13C values are % enrichment (PPP >50%). M. gallisepticum = glycolytic/fermentative homolactic (secretes lactate), opposite of M. bovis (consumes lactate). All rates experimental but directional/relative; no derived qS possible.
